textPlain: I’m not sure where the novelty is in this research. It’s basically reporting something that has been universally known for decades.

Universally known to whoever wanted to intercept that traffic.Maybe and hopefully not known to the staff of those networks (the current staff could be maintaining what somebody else set up) as some of those companies fixed the problem when contacted by the researchers.For sure not known to me and a lot of other people. I believed that everything in digital streams was encrypted. Ok, those ATM connections are probably tech from the 90s, but they probably had upgrades in part because of regulations. Privacy, security, nothing?
